title: Profiling, Event-Loop Health, Memory, Load, and Capacity · Inference Brief: Performance Regression Recovery
summary: A team optimizes microbenchmarks, measures only average latency, ignores coordinated omission and warmup, raises concurrency until throughput peaks, and has no resource or cost model. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsts performance regression recovery increment in performance experiment and capacity envelope. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.
Relate arrival rate, concurrency, service time, queueing, utilization, memory, pool limits, instance count, cost, and headroom without treating estimates as guarantees.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Node and Express server Predict Performance Regression Recovery · Theory Performance Regression Rec…
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 6442 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to gate tail latency with event-loop and utilization capacity evidence; distinguish proxy evidence from origin evidence; then remove one trusted proxy hop and recompute authority from the socket boundary. Browser code models pure decisions only. Node, Express, listener, TLS, PostgreSQL, proxy, worker, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Gate changed builds with noise-aware baselines, representative cases, resource ceilings, overload recovery, rollback thresholds, and retained profiles.
